title: The Barbershop Shave
introKo:
오래된 바버샵에서 받는 뜨거운 수건 면도, 이발사의 손길이 유난히 섬세한 이야기.
…표면적으로는요.
introEn:
A hot-towel shave at the oldest barbershop in town. Mr. Vince has remarkably delicate hands.
...strictly a shave, of course.

quiz.q: 이발사 Mr. Vince가 면도 전에 얼굴에 제일 먼저 올려 준 것은?

0000.name: 조심스러운 첫 손님
0000.en: You asked for gentle and got exactly that. You thank Mr. Vince, pay, and step out with a clean jaw and no plans to return. Some men, he thinks, only need one perfect shave to remember for a lifetime.
0000.ko: 부드럽게 해 달라 했고, 딱 그만큼 받았어요. 당신은 Mr. Vince에게 인사하고 값을 치른 뒤, 말끔한 턱으로 다시 올 계획 없이 나가요. 어떤 남자는 평생 기억할 완벽한 면도 한 번이면 충분하다고, 그는 생각해요.

0001.name: 돌아오는 발걸음
0001.en: You were quiet and careful the whole time, right up until you asked about next week. Now you come every Tuesday, same chair, same hands. "Slow to warm up," Mr. Vince tells the other barbers, "but he always comes back."
0001.ko: 내내 조용하고 조심스러웠죠. 다음 주 얘기를 꺼내기 전까지는요. 이제 당신은 화요일마다 같은 의자, 같은 손을 찾아와요. "데워지는 덴 느려도, 꼭 다시 온다니까." Mr. Vince가 다른 이발사들에게 말해요.

0010.name: 말없이 바짝
0010.en: You barely spoke all afternoon, but when it came to the blade you asked for it as close as it goes. Mr. Vince still tells that story. "The quiet ones," he says, "always want it the closest."
0010.ko: 오후 내내 말수가 거의 없었지만, 면도날 앞에서는 갈 수 있는 만큼 바짝 해 달라 했죠. Mr. Vince는 아직도 그 얘길 해요. "조용한 사람일수록," 그가 말해요, "제일 바짝을 원한다니까."

0011.name: 늦게 뜨거워지는 손님
0011.en: You began shy but ended up asking for the closest shave and a standing appointment. Mr. Vince loves that kind of turn. "Cool at the door, warm in the chair," he says. "My favorite kind of customer."
0011.ko: 수줍게 시작했지만, 끝엔 제일 바짝 해 달라 하고 고정 예약까지 잡았어요. Mr. Vince는 그런 반전을 사랑해요. "문 앞에선 차갑다가 의자에선 뜨거워지는 것," 그가 말해요. "제가 제일 좋아하는 손님이죠."

0100.name: 느긋한 오후
0100.en: You told him to take his time, and he did — every unhurried second of it. Then you thanked him and left, in no rush to book another. "He let me work slow," Mr. Vince says, "and never asked for more. A rare kind of patience."
0100.ko: 천천히 하라 했고, 그는 정말 그랬어요. 서두르지 않는 매 순간까지 전부요. 그러고는 인사만 남기고, 또 예약할 마음 없이 떠났죠. "천천히 하게 두더니, 더는 바라지 않더군요. 보기 드문 여유예요." Mr. Vince가 말해요.

0101.name: 느긋한 단골
0101.en: You never once hurried him, and by the end you had booked next Tuesday too. Now you are the man who lets him take his time, week after week. "The patient ones," he says, "get the best of me every single time."
0101.ko: 그를 한 번도 재촉하지 않았고, 끝엔 다음 화요일까지 예약했어요. 이제 당신은 주마다 그가 느긋하게 일하도록 두는 사람이에요. "여유 있는 사람이," 그가 말해요, "매번 제 최고를 받아 가죠."

0110.name: 조용한 완벽주의자
0110.en: You let him take his time and asked for it as close as a blade can go — then walked out without a word about coming back. Mr. Vince still wonders about you. "That one wanted everything," he says, "and asked for nothing after."
0110.ko: 그가 천천히 하게 두고, 면도날이 갈 수 있는 만큼 바짝 해 달라 했죠. 그러고는 다시 오겠단 말 한마디 없이 나갔어요. Mr. Vince는 아직도 당신이 궁금해요. "그 손님은 다 원하면서," 그가 말해요, "끝나고는 아무것도 청하지 않더군요."

0111.name: 느리게 타오르는 단골
0111.nameEn: Slow to Kindle, Sure to Stay
0111.en: Careful at first, you soon let him take his time, asked for the closest shave, and claimed next week as yours. "Started like a beginner, finished like a regular," Mr. Vince says. "The best ones always do."
0111.ko: 처음엔 조심스럽더니, 곧 그를 느긋하게 두고, 제일 바짝 해 달라 하고, 다음 주까지 당신 것으로 찜했죠. "초보처럼 시작해서 단골처럼 끝났어요," Mr. Vince가 말해요. "최고의 손님들은 늘 그렇죠."

1000.name: 말만 앞선 첫 손님
1000.en: "Do your worst," you said at the door — then went quiet and careful for the rest of it, and left with a simple thank you. Mr. Vince found it charming. "Big words up front," he says, "and a gentleman in the chair."
1000.ko: "마음껏 하세요"라고 문 앞에서 큰소리를 쳤죠. 그러고는 남은 시간 내내 조용하고 조심스러웠고, 짧은 인사만 남기고 갔어요. Mr. Vince는 그게 귀여웠대요. "입구에선 큰소리, 의자에선 신사," 그가 말해요.

1001.name: 약속을 지키는 턱
1001.nameEn: A Man Who Keeps His Word
1001.en: You talked big, sat still and careful, and kept the one promise that mattered — you came back. Now every Tuesday belongs to Mr. Vince and his blade. "Plenty of men talk," he says. "Mine shows up."
1001.ko: 큰소리를 쳤고, 얌전히 조심스럽게 앉아 있었고, 중요한 약속 하나는 지켰어요 — 다시 왔죠. 이제 화요일은 전부 Mr. Vince와 그의 면도날 것이에요. "말만 하는 남자는 많죠," 그가 말해요. "제 손님은 진짜로 와요."

1010.name: 단골 없는 실력자
1010.nameEn: All That Nerve, Gone by Dark
1010.en: You dared him at the door and asked for it as close as steel goes — then left without a word about next time. Mr. Vince shakes his head at the empty chair. "All that nerve," he says, "and I only got him once."
1010.ko: 문 앞에서 그에게 도발하듯 큰소리를 쳤고, 강철이 갈 수 있는 만큼 바짝 해 달라 했죠. 그러고는 다음 얘기 없이 떠났어요. Mr. Vince는 빈 의자를 보며 고개를 저어요. "그 배짱을 가지고," 그가 말해요, "딱 한 번뿐이라니."

1011.name: 떠오르는 단골
1011.en: Bold from the first word, you asked for the closest shave and booked next week before he could even offer. The other barbers keep asking who you are. Mr. Vince just smiles and keeps your chair warm.
1011.ko: 첫마디부터 대담했던 당신은 제일 바짝 해 달라 했고, 그가 권하기도 전에 다음 주를 예약했어요. 다른 이발사들은 당신이 누구냐고 자꾸 물어요. Mr. Vince는 그저 웃으며 당신 의자를 데워 둬요.

1100.name: 큰소리치고 일찍 간 손님
1100.nameEn: Loud at the Door, Gone by Two
1100.en: You dared him at the door and told him to take all the time he wanted — then thanked him and left early, one shave and done. "He talked like a regular," Mr. Vince laughs, "and vanished like a tourist."
1100.ko: 문 앞에서 도발하듯 큰소리를 치고, 원하는 만큼 천천히 하라 했죠. 그러고는 인사만 남기고 일찍 갔어요. 면도 한 번으로 끝이었죠. "단골처럼 말하더니," Mr. Vince가 웃어요, "관광객처럼 사라졌어요."

1101.name: 대담한 단골
1101.en: Bold at the door, in no hurry in the chair, and booked solid for every Tuesday after. The only thing you never did was rush him — and that, he says, is exactly why he saves his sharpest blade for you.
1101.ko: 문 앞에선 대담하게, 의자에선 느긋하게, 그리고 이후 화요일은 전부 예약했어요. 당신이 끝내 하지 않은 단 하나는 그를 재촉하는 것이었죠. 바로 그래서 그가 제일 잘 드는 면도날을 당신을 위해 아껴 둔다고, 그가 말해요.

1110.name: 완벽했던 하루의 손님
1110.en: Bold, unhurried, and asking for the closest shave of your life — you took all he had to give and then walked out for good. Mr. Vince stood a long time by the warm chair. "Some men," he says, "are a perfect afternoon that never comes twice."
1110.ko: 대담하게, 느긋하게, 인생에서 제일 바짝인 면도를 청하며 — 당신은 그가 줄 수 있는 모든 걸 받고는 영영 떠났어요. Mr. Vince는 데워진 의자 곁에 한참 서 있었어요. "어떤 남자는," 그가 말해요, "두 번은 오지 않는 완벽한 오후예요."

1111.name: 소문난 단골
1111.en: By spring, the whole town whispers about Mr. Vince's Tuesday customer — the boldest chair he has ever filled. He tells the other barbers you get the closest shave in the county. And they all notice one thing: your chair is never given to anyone else.
1111.ko: 봄이 되자 온 동네가 Mr. Vince의 화요일 손님 얘기를 수군거려요. 그가 채운 의자 중 가장 대담한 손님이라고요. 그는 다른 이발사들에게 당신이 이 고을에서 제일 바짝인 면도를 받는다고 말해요. 그리고 모두가 한 가지를 눈치채요. 당신 의자는 절대 다른 누구에게도 주어지지 않는다는 걸요.

normal.text: A quiet afternoon. You step into the oldest barbershop in town. The bell rings, and Mr. Vince looks up with a slow, knowing smile.
"A shave, sir?" he asks, brushing off the old leather chair. "Sit. Lean back. From here on, you are in good hands."
He lowers the chair until you are lying almost flat. "First time under a real razor?" he asks. "Don't worry. I am very gentle with beginners."

options.en: Yes, first time. Please be careful.
options.reaction: "Careful is my whole job," he says softly. "Just breathe and let me do the rest."

options.en: Do your worst. I can take it.
options.reaction: "Oh, I never do my worst," he grins. "I do my best. Slowly."

normal.text: He wraps a hot towel around your face, snug and steaming. "Let it soften you up first," he murmurs. "A stiff face fights the blade."
He works up a warm lather and spreads it slow, in small circles. "I never rush a face," he says. "The best part is making you wait for it."

options.en: It feels warmer than I expected.
options.reaction: "Warm opens everything up," he says. "Cold makes a man tense. We don't want that."

options.en: Take all the time you want. I'm not going anywhere.
options.ko: 천천히 하세요. 저 어디 안 가요.
options.reaction: "Good," he says. "The ones who rush always miss the best part."

normal.text: He opens the razor with a soft click. "Now, tilt your head back for me," he says. "Chin up. Bare the throat. That's it."
The first stroke glides down, slow and sure. "Don't move," he whispers. "One slip, and we both remember it. Trust the hands."

options.en: Is it supposed to be this close?
options.reaction: "Close is the whole point," he says. "Anything less, and you would feel cheated."

options.en: Closer. I like it properly clean.
options.ko: 더 바짝요. 저 깔끔한 거 좋아해요.
options.reaction: "A man who knows what he wants," he says. "Hold still. I'll give you the closest of your life."

normal.text: He turns your jaw with two fingers and shaves against the grain. "This is where most men flinch," he says. "You didn't. I'm impressed."
He wipes you clean and pats on the aftershave. It stings, then glows. "Smooth as glass," he says. "Feel that. Not a single hair left."

options.en: That was better than I imagined. Thank you.
options.reaction: "Beginners always say that," he smiles. "Wait until the second time."

options.en: Same chair, same hands — next week?
options.ko: 같은 의자, 같은 손 — 다음 주에 또요?
options.reaction: "My regulars never sit anywhere else," he says. "I'll keep this chair warm for you."

normal.text: You step back into the street, jaw smooth, face glowing. Some habits, you decide, are worth keeping for life.

hard.text: A quiet, rainy afternoon draws you into the oldest barbershop in town, where Mr. Vince glances up from his chair with a slow, knowing smile.
"A shave, sir?" he asks, dusting off the worn leather chair. "Sit down and lean back. From this moment on, you are entirely in my hands."
He tips the chair back until you are lying almost flat, studying your face. "Your first time under a real razor?" he asks. "Don't fret. I am always exceptionally gentle with beginners."

options.en: Yes, my first time. Please be gentle.
options.reaction: "Being gentle is my entire craft," he says softly. "Just breathe, and leave the rest to me."

options.en: Do your worst. I can take whatever you give.
options.ko: 마음껏 하세요. 뭐든 다 받아 줄게요.
options.reaction: "Oh, I never do my worst," he grins. "I do my finest work — and I take my time doing it."

hard.text: He winds a steaming towel around your face, snug and fragrant. "Let it soften you up first," he murmurs. "A stiff face only fights the blade, and I dislike a fight."
He whips up a warm lather and spreads it in slow little circles, taking his time. "I never rush a face like yours," he says. "Half the pleasure is making a man wait for it."

options.en: It feels far warmer than I expected.
options.reaction: "Warmth opens everything up," he explains. "Cold makes a man tense, and tension is the enemy here."

options.en: Take all the time you like. I'm in no hurry at all.
options.ko: 천천히 하세요. 저 하나도 안 급해요.
options.reaction: "Good," he says. "The men who rush are the ones who always miss the best part."

hard.text: He unfolds the razor with a soft, deliberate click. "Now tilt your head back for me," he says. "Chin up, throat bared. Yes — exactly like that."
The first stroke glides down your neck, slow and certain. "Don't move a muscle," he whispers. "One slip is something we would both remember. Trust the hands."

options.en: Is it meant to be quite this close?
options.reaction: "Close is the entire point," he says. "Anything less, and you would leave feeling cheated."

options.en: Closer, please. I like it properly clean.
options.ko: 더 바짝요. 저 깔끔한 거 좋아하거든요.
options.reaction: "A man who knows exactly what he wants," he murmurs. "Hold perfectly still — I'll give you the closest shave of your life."

hard.text: He tilts your jaw with two fingers and draws the blade against the grain. "This is the moment most men flinch," he observes. "You didn't so much as blink. I'm impressed."
He wipes you clean and presses on the aftershave, which stings sharply before it glows. "Smooth as glass," he says. "Run your hand along it. Not a single hair left behind."

options.en: That was far better than I imagined. Thank you.
options.reaction: "The beginners always say exactly that," he smiles. "Just wait until the second time."

options.en: Same chair, same hands — shall I come back next week?
options.ko: 같은 의자, 같은 손 — 다음 주에 또 올까요?
options.reaction: "My regulars never sit in anyone else's chair," he says. "I'll keep this one warm for you."

hard.text: You step out into the street with your jaw smooth and your face quietly glowing, deciding that some habits are well worth keeping for the rest of your life.

wink.stiff: "뻣뻣하면 면도날과 싸운다"는 말은 면도의 상식이에요. 그런데 왜 이렇게 다르게 들릴까요.
wink.soften: "먼저 좀 풀어 드릴게요"는 뜨거운 수건이 하는 일이에요. 그 말을 굳이 고른 건 이발사고요.
wink.close: "바짝"은 이발소 안에서는 최고의 자랑이에요. 이발소 밖에서는 대개 다른 거리를 뜻하죠.
wink.throat: 면도하려면 목을 드러내야 하는 건 사실이에요. "목을 내어 주세요"라는 말이 유난히 다정하게 들리는 것도 사실이고요.
wink.grain: "결을 거슬러" 미는 건 제일 바짝 미는 방법이에요. 남자들이 움찔하는 지점이라는 말도, 순수하게 면도 얘기예요. 아마도요.
wink.smooth: "유리처럼 매끈하다"는 잘된 면도의 최고 칭찬이에요. 손을 대 보라는 권유까지가 한 세트죠.
wink.slip: "한 번 미끄러지면 둘 다 기억할 거예요"는 면도날 조심하라는 경고예요. 경고인데 왜 약속처럼 들릴까요.

winkEn.stiff: "A stiff face fights the blade" is ordinary barbering wisdom. So why does it land so differently here?
winkEn.soften: "Let it soften you up first" is simply what a hot towel does. Choosing those exact words, however, was entirely his idea.
winkEn.close: "Close" is a barber's proudest word — inside the shop. Out on the street it tends to mean a rather different kind of distance.
winkEn.throat: To shave a man you truly must bare his throat. It is also true that "bare the throat" has rarely sounded quite so tender.
winkEn.grain: Going "against the grain" is how you get the closest possible shave. The line about where men flinch is, of course, purely about shaving. Probably.
winkEn.smooth: "Smooth as glass" is the highest praise a shave can earn — and the invitation to run your hand along it comes bundled right in.
winkEn.slip: "One slip, and we both remember it" is a warning to hold still near a blade. A warning that somehow arrives sounding like a promise.

after.en: later than; when something is finished

afternoon.en: the time between noon and evening

aftershave.en: lotion a man puts on after a shave

against.ko: ~을 거슬러, ~에 맞서 (against the grain: 결을 거슬러)

along.ko: ~을 따라 (run your hand along: 손으로 쓸어 보다)

appointment.en: a set time to meet or be served

barbers.en: people who cut hair and give shaves

barbershop.en: a shop where men get haircuts and shaves

beginner.en: a person just starting to learn

belongs.en: is owned by; is part of

blade.en: the sharp edge of a razor or knife

blink.en: to shut and open your eyes fast

book.en: to reserve a time or place

breathe.en: to take air in and out

brushing.en: sweeping something off with light strokes

cheated.ko: 속은 (feel cheated: 손해 본 기분이 들다)

chin.en: the part of the face below the mouth

claimed.en: took as your own (past of claim)

clean.en: with nothing dirty or extra left

county.en: an area of land, like a district

craft.en: a skill you do well with your hands

customer.en: a person who buys or is served

do.en: to make or perform an action

expected.en: thought it would happen (past of expect)

far.en: by a great amount; a long way

feels.en: gives a certain feeling to the touch

fingers.en: the parts at the end of your hand

flinch.en: to pull back quickly from fear or pain

found.en: thought it was; discovered (past of find)

glowing.en: shining with a soft, warm light

glows.en: shines with a soft, warm light

grain.ko: 결 (against the grain: 털이 난 결을 거슬러)
grain.en: the natural direction the hair lies

grins.en: smiles in a big, playful way

habits.en: things you do often without thinking

hair.en: the fine threads that grow on skin

hurried.en: went too fast; rushed (past of hurry)

imagined.en: pictured in your mind (past of imagine)

jaw.en: the lower part of the face

lean.en: to rest your body against or back

leave.ko: 떠나다, 남겨 두다 (leave the rest: 나머지를 맡기다)
leave.en: to go away; to let stay

lifetime.en: the whole length of a life

lying.en: resting flat, as on a bed

meant.en: supposed to be; intended (past of mean)

miss.en: to fail to get or reach

most.en: the greatest number; more than all

mr.en: a title before a man's name

muscle.ko: 근육 (not move a muscle: 꼼짝도 않다)
muscle.en: the body part that makes you move

neck.en: the part joining head and body

nerve.en: the courage to do something bold

notice.en: to see and become aware of

offer.en: to ask if someone wants something

ones.ko: ~한 사람들, 것들 (the quiet ones: 조용한 사람들)

patience.en: the calm to wait without rushing

point.ko: 핵심, 요점 (the whole point: 바로 그게 핵심)

promise.en: words you say you will keep

regular.en: a customer who comes back often

second.en: coming after the first; a short moment

shakes.ko: 흔들다 (shake의 3인칭; shake one's head: 고개를 젓다)

shall.en: used to offer or ask about doing

shave.en: cutting hair off the face with a blade

shaves.en: cuts hair off with a blade

single.ko: 단 하나의 (not a single: 단 하나도 없이)
single.en: just one; not any at all

sir.en: a polite word for a man

soften.ko: 부드럽게 하다, 풀어 주다 (soften up: 긴장을 풀어 주다)

spreads.en: puts a thin layer over something

stood.en: was on his feet (past of stand)

stroke.en: one smooth movement of the blade

supposed.ko: ~하기로 되어 있다 (be supposed to: 원래 그래야 하다)
supposed.en: meant to be a certain way

taking.ko: 쓰다, 들이다 (take one's time: 여유를 갖다)

tilt.en: to lean something to one side or back

tourist.ko: 관광객 (한 번 오고 마는 사람 비유)
tourist.en: a visitor who comes only once

turns.en: moves something to a new position

which.en: a word that links to a thing

whips.ko: 휘저어 거품을 내다 (whip up: 거품을 내다)

work.en: a task; to do a task

worst.ko: 최악 (do your worst: 마음껏 해 봐라)
worst.en: the most extreme thing you can do

yes.en: a word to agree or say so
